Stations.Name: Fresno/F.S.U. USDA
Stations.SitingDesc: This station was the first CIMIS station and was active for under six consecutive years.

Stations.Name: FivePoints
Stations.SitingDesc:
This station is located on a USDA research facility located in the Southern San Joaquin Valley. Consequently the surrounding environment can be subject to land use changes.
Prevailing Wind: North

Stations.Name: Parlier
Stations.SitingDesc:
DATE: 11-12-02

STATION#:39
STATION NAME: Parlier
ETO ZONE: 12
PREVAILING WINDS: NW
LOCAL CHARACTER: Primarily agricultural area. Large fields of cotton, grapes and orchards characterize regional farming operations.

DESCRIPTION OF STATION SITE:
Located on a University of California research facility. The station is in a flood-irrigated plot with a mixed clover/grass cover.

NORTH:
100ft: Grass-clover
100-300+ ft: Research plots and instrumentation.

EAST:
75ft: Grass-clover
75ft: Orchard

SOUTH:
100ft: Grass-clover
100-125ft: Paved access road
125ft: Research plot with grapes

WEST:
100ft: Grass-clover
100-200ft: Paved landing strip and aircraft parking area.

COMMENTS:
The area around the station plot is subject to change as the requirements of the research facility change.

Stations.Name: Los Banos
Stations.SitingDesc:
DATE: 11-12-02

STATION#:56
STATION NAME: Los Banos
ETO ZONE: 15
PREVAILING WINDS: NW
LOCAL CHARACTER: Primarily agricultural area. Large fields of cotton, grapes and orchards characterize regional farming operations.

DESCRIPTION OF STATION SITE:
Located on a large flood irrigated pasture. The grass is primarily clumpy fescue that is grazed to a general height of approximately 12ft. The station is surrounded with a Critter Corral type of enclosure.

NORTH:
300+ft: Grass pasture

EAST:
1200+ft; Grass pasture

SOUTH:
300+ft: Grass pasture

WEST:
400+ft: Grass pasture

COMMENTS:

Stations.Name: Merced
Stations.SitingDesc:
DATE: 11-18-02

STATION#:148
STATION NAME: Merced
ETO ZONE: 12
PREVAILING WINDS: NW
LOCAL CHARACTER: Rural county with primarily agricultural interests. Common farming activities include tomatoes, cow-calf operations, dairy, stone fruits and almonds.

DESCRIPTION OF STATION SITE:
Located on a private farm in a flood-irrigated pasture. It was installed in a fenced enclosure for protection from livestock. The pasture could be better utilized, as it is sometimes tall and uneven.

NORTH:
150ft: Pasture
150ft: Bear Creek
150ft: Walnuts (NE)
150ft: Alfalfa/mixed pasture (NW)

EAST:
200+ ft: Pasture
200+ ft: Plums

SOUTH:
120ft: Pasture
120ft: Gravel parking area (SW)
120-200ft: Cement Slab (SE)
200+ ft: Farm buildings (SE)

WEST:
180ft: Pasture
180+ ft: Walnuts

COMMENTS:

Stations.Name: Oakland Foothills
Stations.SitingDesc:
DATE: 11-18-02

STATION #: 149
STATION NAME: Oakland Foothills
ETO ZONE: 1
PREVAILING WINDS: W
LOCAL CHARACTER: Located on the campus of Mills College, the site is adjacent to a densely urbanized area.

DESCRIPTION OF STATION SITE:
It is located on a small turfed area surrounded by the college buildings and other facilities. The site slopes (approximately 30%) NESW

NORTHEAST:
25ft: Turf
20-35ft: Vegetable garden
35-110ft: Parking and street
110-140ft: Grass and shrubs
140ft: Two story building

SOUTHEAST:
25ft: Turf
25-40ft: Grass and shrubs
40ft: Two story building and one story building

SOUTHWEST:
100ft: Turf
100-120ft: Grass, shrubs and trees (30` redwood)
120ft: One-story classrooms

NORTHWEST:
25ft: Turf
25-50ft: Flower garden, grass and shrubs
50ft: One-story dormitories

COMMENTS:
This site is significantly constrained when it is compared to the CIMIS siting criteria. ETo and other environmental data should be used with caution.
